summary: This notebook serves as a personal benchmark and tutorial for the `kbench` library, demonstrating how to define an LLM evaluation task, prompt a model, and validate responses using both hard keyword assertions and an LLM-as-a-judge framework. It includes a brief exploratory component that loads a Tupac Shakur lyrics dataset, renames columns, and generates a word cloud visualization. The author explicitly notes that further development was halted due to environment installation conflicts. Readers should understand how to structure automated LLM evaluations and leverage helper libraries for benchmarking workflows.
competition_flow: Defines an LLM benchmark task using `kbench`, prompts a model to answer a question, evaluates the response with assertions, and briefly loads/visualizes a lyrics dataset before halting due to environment installation conflicts.
data_reading: Loads a CSV file (`lyrics-TUPAC.csv`) using `pd.read_csv` and renames columns to `['artist', 'title', 'lyrics']`.
insights: Automated LLM evaluation can be structured using both deterministic keyword checks and LLM-as-a-judge criteria for nuanced quality assessment.
The `kbench` library provides a streamlined interface for defining benchmark tasks and programmatically validating model outputs.
Environment dependency management can quickly halt notebook execution, as seen when installing `torch` caused failures.
